releaseNotes: v0.28.0 — Smarter returns, plus three nav tweaks Alex asked for. (1) Return eligibility: the Expiring Medications Hub now flags each bottle's return-for-credit status at a glance — controlled substances and opened bottles are flagged 'not returnable,' anything with under 90 days of shelf life remaining is also flagged 'not returnable' (the wholesaler won't credit it), and topicals get a 'verify policy' note. The Pull-confirm dialog repeats the verdict so the tech sees it before packing. Mark a bottle opened with the new 'Bottle opened' toggle on the Edit Bottle dialog. Local database schema moves to v20, adding the per-bottle opened_at timestamp; existing inventory upgrades automatically. (2) Two new top-nav buttons next to Activity and Reports: Daily Intake (a focused scan-and-list screen for the day's incoming orders) and Expiring Medications (promoted from the overflow menu). (3) Smarter labels for non-2D bottles: a label printed for a bottle without a manufacturer 2D barcode now carries a unique RxRescue Code 128 — re-scanning the printed label finds the same bottle with its expiration, lot, and history. (4) Grouped zones now audit as one scan run: tap a group, scan once, and every zone in the group ticks off together. (5) Sync hardening: a 400-rejected sync op no longer retries forever (the May 19 incident class), and a new CI gate catches payload-key drift between client and server before release — caught and fixed a latent bug that would have 400'd every received-date edit. 545 tests passing.
